stops.name: City Hall Square (Rådhuspladsen)
stops.shortDescription: The bustling civic heart of the city, dominated by the highly ornate City Hall. A golden statue of Bishop Absalon, the legendary founder of Copenhagen, overlooks the square.

stops.name: Strøget
stops.shortDescription: One of the longest pedestrian shopping streets in Europe, spanning 1.1 kilometers. It was controversially closed to cars in 1962, sparking a pedestrian-friendly revolution in urban planning.

stops.name: Rundetaarn (Round Tower)
stops.shortDescription: A 17th-century tower built as an astronomical observatory. Uniquely, it lacks stairs; instead, a wide, spiraling equestrian ramp allows horses and carriages to ride all the way to the top.

stops.name: Christiansborg Palace
stops.shortDescription: Located on the tiny island of Slotsholmen, it uniquely houses all three supreme parts of the Danish government. You can view the ruins of the original 1167 castle excavated directly beneath the current palace.

stops.name: Børsen (Old Stock Exchange)
stops.shortDescription: A stunning Dutch Renaissance building commissioned by King Christian IV. It is most famous for its distinctive spire, formed by the intertwined tails of four stone dragons.

stops.name: Nyhavn
stops.shortDescription: The iconic 17th-century waterfront canal lined with brightly colored townhouses and historical wooden ships. Fairy tale author Hans Christian Andersen lived in several different houses along this canal throughout his life.

stops.name: Amalienborg
stops.shortDescription: The winter residence of the Danish royal family, consisting of four identical classical palace façades surrounding an octagonal courtyard. The changing of the Royal Guard takes place here daily at noon.

stops.name: Frederik's Church (The Marble Church)
stops.shortDescription: Perfectly aligned with the Amalienborg Palace square, this magnificent church boasts the largest church dome in all of Scandinavia, resting heavily on 12 massive marble columns.

stops.name: Designmuseum Danmark
stops.shortDescription: Housed beautifully in a former Rococo hospital, this museum showcases the world-renowned history of Danish design, from famous Mid-Century Modern chairs to contemporary industrial aesthetics.

stops.name: Churchillparken
stops.shortDescription: A serene, green public park named in honor of Winston Churchill and the British assistance during the liberation of Denmark in World War II. It features several solemn war memorials.

stops.name: Gefion Fountain
stops.shortDescription: A spectacular, large-scale fountain depicting the Norse goddess Gefion fiercely driving four oxen. Legend says she plowed the island of Zealand out of Sweden to create Denmark.

stops.name: Kastellet
stops.shortDescription: One of the absolute best-preserved star fortresses in Northern Europe. Constructed in 1626, its steep earthwork ramparts now serve as a beautiful public park featuring an iconic historic red windmill.

stops.name: The Little Mermaid
stops.shortDescription: Perched quietly on a wet rock by the waterside, this modest bronze statue is based on the famous fairy tale by Hans Christian Andersen. It has become the most iconic and photographed symbol of Copenhagen.

stops.name: Langelinie Promenade
stops.shortDescription: A beautiful, breezy waterfront promenade extending from the Little Mermaid. It is the perfect place for watching massive cruise ships, locals walking their dogs, and taking in the fresh Baltic Sea air.
